name: AGI Job · Explain Smart Contract 1 & 2 for a Newbie (High-Level)
description: Write a clear, accessible, high-level explanation of two AGIJobManager ecosystem smart contracts for a complete newcomer to Web3 and smart contracts. Contract 1 is AGIJobManager (0xB3AAeb69b630f0299791679c063d68d6687481d1) — the core job marketplace and settlement engine. Contract 2 is AGIJobDiscoveryPrime (0xd5EF1dde7Ac60488f697ff2A7967a52172A78F29) — the premium hiring/discovery layer that sits on top. The deliverable must be a single Markdown file that explains both contracts in plain language, with analogies and a clear diagram or structured walkthrough of how they interact. No Solidity code blocks or deep technical jargon. Suitable for someone who has never read a smart contract before.

properties.title: Explain Smart Contract 1 & 2 for a Newbie (High-Level)
properties.summary: Write a clear, accessible, high-level explanation of the two core AGIJobManager ecosystem smart contracts for a complete newcomer. Contract 1 (AGIJobManager) is the job marketplace and settlement engine. Contract 2 (AGIJobDiscoveryPrime) is the premium hiring/discovery layer. The deliverable is a single polished Markdown file in plain English with analogies, no Solidity code, and a structured walkthrough of both contracts and how they interact.
properties.details: This job is part of the AGIJobManager ecosystem documentation initiative. The goal is to produce a public-facing explainer that any newcomer can read and understand — someone who may never have interacted with a smart contract before. The explainer must cover both contracts at a conceptual level, explain the roles (Employer, Agent, Validator), explain how $AGIALPHA is used as a utility token in the system, and walk through the lifecycle of a job end-to-end. The final file must be hosted on IPFS and referenced in the Job Completion URI metadata.

contract1.name: AGIJobManager
contract1.role: Core job marketplace and settlement engine. Handles job creation, escrow, agent assignment, validator voting, dispute resolution, and payout. Mints an ERC-721 NFT on job completion.
contract1.token: $AGIALPHA (ERC-20) — used for job escrow, payouts, agent bonds, validator bonds, dispute bonds
highlightedFeatures: Employer posts a job and locks $AGIALPHA payout in escrow
Agent applies (must hold qualifying NFT) and posts a performance bond
Validators vote approve/disapprove and post bonds per vote
Dispute resolution via Moderators or Owner if validators deadlock
Completion mints an NFT to the winning agent
Remainder after agent/validator payouts stays in contract as platform revenue

contract2.name: AGIJobDiscoveryPrime
contract2.role: Premium discovery/hiring layer that sits on top of AGIJobManager. Implements a full competitive multi-stage hiring process: commit-reveal applications, shortlisting, trial submissions, blind validator scoring, winner designation.
contract2.relationship: AGIJobDiscoveryPrime calls AGIJobManager to create jobs and designate the winning agent. It does NOT replace AGIJobManager — it is an optional premium intake layer.
highlightedFeatures: Employer creates a 'premium' job with a structured procurement process
Agents submit blinded commitments (to prevent front-running), then reveal applications
Top applicants are shortlisted by historical reputation score
Shortlisted finalists accept, post a stake, and submit a trial work sample
Validators commit and reveal blind scores for each finalist
Winner is computed using a weighted composite of historical + trial scores
Fallback finalist promotion if the winner does not accept
Discovery Stats system penalises agents who ghost (don't reveal, don't submit trials)

deliverables: One polished English Markdown file explaining both contracts for a newbie at a high level
The file must cover: what each contract does, the roles, how $AGIALPHA is used, the job lifecycle end-to-end, and how the two contracts interact
No raw Solidity code — plain English with analogies and optional ASCII/Markdown diagrams
One public IPFS URI resolving directly to the final Markdown file
One Job Completion URI (metadata JSON IPFS URI) that clearly points to the final Markdown file
acceptanceCriteria: The deliverable is a single polished Markdown file in English
Both Contract 1 (AGIJobManager) and Contract 2 (AGIJobDiscoveryPrime) are covered
The explanation is accessible to someone with no prior smart contract experience
The roles of Employer, Agent, and Validator are clearly explained
$AGIALPHA is accurately described as a utility token (not equity, not investment)
The basic job lifecycle (post → apply → complete → validate → settle) is explained step by step
The relationship between Contract 1 and Contract 2 is clearly explained
The tone is friendly, clear, and avoids unnecessary jargon
A public IPFS URI resolves directly to the Markdown file
A Job Completion URI resolves to a metadata JSON that clearly points to the Markdown file
requirements: Verified eligible agent ENS path (agent.agi.eth or alpha.agent.agi.eth)
Qualifying AGI NFT payout tier so applyForJob does not revert
Familiarity with Ethereum smart contracts, ERC-20, ERC-721, and escrow mechanics at a conceptual level
Ability to translate complex protocol mechanics into simple, accurate analogies
Ability to publish stable IPFS artifacts (final Markdown file and completion metadata JSON)
Willingness to submit the Job Completion URI and have the result publicly validated on-chain
